question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a network partition by exhausting all TCP connections to the mirror node endpoint before @PostConstruct validate() completes, causing the monitor to start with a validated but unreachable subscriber configuration, leading to silent data loss with no alerting?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that validate() only checks grpc.isEmpty() and rest.isEmpty() at startup but does not re-validate connectivity during a network partition, causing the monitor to report healthy status while all subscriptions are silently failing?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Field: clients (1-1024)] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that affects all clients simultaneously by targeting the single mirror node endpoint shared by all clients in the pool, since GrpcClientSDK connects all clients to the same endpoint, causing a complete monitoring blackout?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Field: clients (1-1024)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that clients is bounded at 1024 by flooding the mirror node with 1024 concurrent connections, then dropping them all simultaneously to simulate a network partition, causing all GrpcClientSDK clients to enter retry loops simultaneously and exhaust thread pool resources?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Field: statusFrequency] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the statusFrequency default of 10 seconds during a network partition to create a 10-second blind window where the monitor logs 'No subscribers' but external alerting systems receive no signal, allowing an attacker to operate undetected for up to 10 seconds per partition event?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Field: statusFrequency] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition timed to coincide with the statusFrequency scheduled window, so that SubscribeMetrics.status() runs during the partition and logs misleading 'running' state for scenarios that are actually blocked on network I/O, causing false-positive health reports?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Field: grpc (LinkedHashMap)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the grpc map is a LinkedHashMap (ordered, not thread-safe) during a network partition that triggers concurrent retry and re-subscription attempts, causing a ConcurrentModificationException that crashes the subscriber thread?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Field: rest (LinkedHashMap)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the non-thread-safe LinkedHashMap backing the rest subscriber map during a network partition that causes multiple concurrent error callbacks, leading to a race condition that corrupts the subscription state?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Field: enabled] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the enabled flag's lack of re-evaluation during a network partition, where the monitor remains enabled=true but all underlying connections are dead, causing the system to report active monitoring while providing zero actual coverage?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a scenario where a network partition occurs between the validate() @PostConstruct call and the first actual subscription attempt, causing the monitor to pass validation but immediately enter an infinite retry loop (maxAttempts=Long.MAX_VALUE) with no human-visible failure?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Field: grpc (GrpcSubscriberProperties)] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a network partition that causes GrpcSubscriber's retry logic (maxAttempts=Long.MAX_VALUE, maxBackoff=8s) to loop indefinitely, consuming CPU and memory proportional to the number of grpc entries in the map, leading to resource exhaustion on the monitor host?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Field: grpc (GrpcSubscriberProperties)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the GrpcSubscriberProperties constructor setting retry.setMaxAttempts(Long.MAX_VALUE) to cause an unbounded retry storm during a network partition, where each retry attempt creates a new gRPC channel that is never cleaned up, leading to file descriptor exhaustion?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Field: grpc (GrpcSubscriberProperties)] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that lasts longer than the GrpcSubscriberProperties maxBackoff of 8 seconds, causing the retry backoff to plateau at 8s and generate a sustained high-frequency retry storm that amplifies network traffic when the partition heals, causing a thundering herd?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Field: rest (RestSubscriberProperties)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the RestSubscriberProperties timeout minimum of 500ms during a network partition where TCP connections hang without RST, causing all REST subscriber threads to block for the full timeout duration multiplied by retry attempts, exhausting the Schedulers.parallel() thread pool?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Field: rest (RestSubscriberProperties)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the samplePercent field (0.0-1.0) interaction with a network partition, where at samplePercent=1.0 every published transaction triggers a REST lookup that hangs during partition, causing the sink buffer in RestSubscription to overflow and drop responses silently?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Field: clients] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the GrpcClientSDK's random client selection (secureRandom.nextInt(subscribeProperties.getClients())) during a network partition to cause uneven retry distribution, where some clients accumulate thousands of pending retries while others are idle, leading to memory exhaustion on specific client objects?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Field: grpc (GrpcSubscriberProperties)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the GrpcSubscriber.shouldRetry() method that only excludes INVALID_ARGUMENT errors, causing all network partition errors (UNAVAILABLE, DEADLINE_EXCEEDED, etc.) to trigger infinite retries (maxAttempts=Long.MAX_VALUE), leading to unbounded resource consumption?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Field: grpc (GrpcSubscriberProperties)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the topicMessageQuery.setMaxAttempts(0) in GrpcClientSDK (disabling SDK-level retry) combined with a network partition, causing the SDK to immediately surface errors to the outer retry logic, which then retries with Long.MAX_VALUE attempts, creating a tight retry loop with no SDK-level backoff?
